sections.title: A: Gap Fill (High-Speed Rail Text)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Europe's first high-speed rail service (1) ...... launched in France in 1981, when the TGV began operating between Paris and Lyon at speeds previously unimaginable on rail.
The train runs (2) ...... electricity supplied by a dedicated high-voltage network and is capable of reaching speeds in excess of three hundred kilometres per hour.
On-board comfort (3) ...... maintained throughout the journey by an advanced climate-control system, which automatically adjusts the temperature in (4) ...... carriage.
When the train passes through a tunnel at speed, the pressure-management system compensates automatically, (5) ...... this way preventing any discomfort to passengers.
If the train detects an obstruction on the line ahead, the emergency braking system activates (6) ...... to bring the service to a controlled stop.
Once you are (7) ...... the reservation platform, each leg of your journey (8) ...... be updated or rebooked with a single click.
(9) ...... are reported to be a number of ambitious expansion projects currently under development across Southern and Eastern Europe.
Several governments and transport authorities consider (10) ...... to be the most important investment in sustainable infrastructure of the coming decade.

sections.title: B: Word Formation
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: The veteran jazz musician disappeared from the public eye for nearly a decade before staging a remarkable ...... with a sell-out world tour. **COME**
The hiker was visibly exhausted and walking rather ...... along the cliff-top path by the time the rescue team reached him. **STEADY**
The tribute paid to the retiring headteacher at the end-of-year assembly was one of the most ...... speeches I have ever been privileged to hear. **MOVE**
At the far end of the lane, a narrow cobbled ...... led through the old city wall and into the cathedral quarter beyond. **PASS**
The committee has proposed a package of ...... reforms designed to improve working conditions and reduce the gender pay gap. **PROGRESS**
Years of chronic mismanagement and political upheaval had thoroughly ...... the entire regional economy. **STABLE**
The team went on to win the championship, the injuries to their three key players ...... . **STAND**
The motorist was caught by a roadside camera and issued with her second ...... penalty of the year. **SPEED**

sections.title: C: Multiple Choice (Circle the correct word)

items.sentence: Would you be able to **make / run / do** the new intern a quick induction tour of the building this afternoon?
The coach was careful not to **lift / raise / boost** the players' expectations too soon before such a difficult fixture.
Arguing about the font size of the report is really beside the **point / subject / theme** — what matters is whether the content is accurate.
When the director offered her a leading role in the film, she **moved / jumped / ran** at the chance without a moment's hesitation.
His lecture was thorough and engaging, but I found the theoretical section in the middle rather difficult to **track / follow / watch**.
The project seemed impossibly complex at first, but by the final week everything had **fell / went / came** into place.
We were sitting on the terrace enjoying the evening when the breeze started to **go / turn / get** distinctly cold, so we moved indoors.
Millions of people across the country **go / get / become** without adequate heating during the coldest months, and that is a serious concern.

sections.title: D: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

items.sentence:
López slipped the ball to García in the closing stages of the first half. **By**
García ...... López in the closing stages of the first half.

Insiders say that auditors have identified a number of irregularities since the audit began. **reported**
There ...... a number of irregularities since the audit began.

Try to keep pace with the leading riders throughout the opening stage of the race. **fall**
Try ...... the leading riders throughout the opening stage of the race.

The police sealed off the area around the abandoned warehouse for safety reasons. **cordoned**
The area around the abandoned warehouse ...... police for safety reasons.
items.answer: has been cordoned off by the

items.sentence:
A local decorator came and repainted our kitchen for us last spring. **had**
We ...... a local decorator last spring.

The department head always likes to thank staff personally on significant professional milestones. **point**
The department head always ...... staff personally on significant professional milestones.

After a hesitant opening, the audience were soon cheering at the comedian's routines. **had**
After a hesitant opening, the comedian ...... at their routines.

I just need to deal with this email quickly and then I'll be right with you. **way**
I just need to ...... and then I'll be right with you.
items.answer: get this email out of the way
